title: NOC 31302 – Nurse Practitioners | GTR Immigration
description: Nurse Practitioners under NOC 31302 are advanced practice nurses playing a critical role in Canada’s primary and acute healthcare systems. With expanding healthcare access programs and physician shortages in many regions of Canada, Nurse Practitioners (NPs) are among the most sought-after healthcare professionals nationwide. As a TEER 1 occupation, NOC 31302 is eligible under Express Entry and various Provincial Nominee Programs, making it a strong pathway toward Canada PR for qualified professionals.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER1 – University degree requiredMajor Group31 – Professional occupations in healthSub-Major Group313 – Nursing and allied health professionalsMinor Group3130 – Nursing and allied health professionalsNOC Code31302

duties: Nurse Practitioners perform some or all of the following duties:
Examine patients and take detailed medical histories
Order and interpret laboratory tests and diagnostic imaging
Provide health promotion and disease prevention guidance
Coordinate and manage primary patient care
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in nursing (NP program) or
Registered nurse qualification with advanced/extended primary healthcare education
Several years of experience as a registered nurse
Mandatory registration with a provincial or territorial regulatory body
British Columbia College of Nurses and Midwives
College of Registered Nurses of Alberta
Ordre des infirmières et infirmiers du Québec
Internationally educated Nurse Practitioners must complete credential recognition and licensing processes before practicing.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $58,100 to $121,300, depending on province, specialization, and level of experience.
job_outlook: Government investments in healthcare workforce expansion continue to support demand for Nurse Practitioners across Canada.
labour_market_demand: This near balance indicates stable national demand, with particularly strong opportunities in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Manitoba, and Nova Scotia.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Nurse Practitioner?
TEER 1 eligibility (high CRS value under Express Entry)
Direct PR pathways for healthcare occupations
Canada continues expanding access to primary care, especially in underserved rural and northern communities.
pathways: If you are a qualified Nurse Practitioner, NOC 31302 may provide a strong pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your structured pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 31302.
